Meet Queso!

Our beloved bearded dragon who brings joy, wonder, and countless learning opportunities to our family. Discover the fascinating world of these incredible reptiles through Queso's journey.

About Our Scaly Friend

Queso's Story

Queso joined our family as a young hatchling and has grown into a magnificent, gentle giant. Named after our love for cheese (and his golden coloration), Queso has become not just a pet, but a beloved family member who teaches us daily about the wonders of reptilian behavior and care.

Bearded dragons, scientifically known as Pogona, are native to Australia and make exceptional pets due to their docile nature and unique personalities. Queso exemplifies all the best traits of his species - curious, calm, and surprisingly affectionate.

Through Queso, we've learned about proper nutrition, habitat management, and the importance of UVB lighting for reptilian health. His presence has enriched our understanding of these remarkable creatures.

Amazing Bearded Dragon Facts

🌡️

Temperature Masters

Bearded dragons are excellent thermoregulators! They can change their skin color from light to dark to absorb or reflect heat, just like Queso does when basking.

👁️

Third Eye Vision

They have a photosensitive "third eye" on top of their head called a parietal eye that detects shadows and changes in light - perfect for spotting aerial predators!

💤

Brumation Sleepers

Like bears hibernate, bearded dragons brumate! They can sleep for weeks or months during cooler periods, barely moving or eating.

🏃‍♂️

Speed Demons

Despite their calm demeanor, bearded dragons can run up to 9 miles per hour and even run on their hind legs when reaching top speed!

🌊

Swimming Champions

Excellent swimmers! They can hold their breath underwater and use their tail as a rudder. Queso loves his occasional supervised bath time.

🦎

Arm Waving Hello

That adorable arm wave isn't just cute - it's communication! It's a submissive gesture that says "I'm not a threat" to other beardies.

Bearded Dragon Care Guide

🏠 Habitat Requirements

  • 40-gallon tank minimum for adults (75+ gallons preferred)
  • Basking spot temperature: 95-110°F
  • Cool side temperature: 75-85°F
  • UVB lighting for 10-12 hours daily
  • Proper substrate (no loose sand for juveniles)
  • Hiding spots and climbing branches

🥬 Diet & Nutrition

  • Juveniles: 80% insects, 20% vegetables
  • Adults: 20% insects, 80% vegetables
  • Safe vegetables: collard greens, squash, bell peppers
  • Protein: crickets, dubia roaches, black soldier fly larvae
  • Calcium supplementation with D3
  • Fresh water available daily

🏥 Health & Wellness

  • Regular vet checkups with reptile specialists
  • Watch for signs of metabolic bone disease
  • Monitor for respiratory infections
  • Proper shedding assistance when needed
  • Maintain proper humidity levels (30-40%)
  • Quarantine new dragons for 30+ days

🎭 Behavior & Handling

  • Allow new dragons time to acclimate
  • Support their body when handling
  • Watch for stress signs: black beard, glass surfing
  • Respect their sleep cycle and brumation
  • Provide mental stimulation with exploration time
  • Understand their body language and moods
